text.title: Surviving Mutants Loop into the Agent
text.summary: Every CI failure pipes the surviving-mutants list back to the agent until the score clears or the agent stops.

section.title: Close the loop, not the eyes
section.p: A surviving mutant is the strongest possible feedback for an agent: a small, deterministic source diff that did not break a single test. Capture the mutation report (Stryker JSON, mutmut output, ACH report) and pass it to the next agent turn as context, asking the agent to add assertions that kill specific mutants — not to "improve coverage." Stop only when the score crosses the threshold or the agent declares an equivalent mutant with justification.

rule.statement: When mutation CI fails, the next agent turn MUST receive (a) the list of surviving mutants with file, line, mutator name, and original-vs-mutated source, and (b) the explicit instruction "add or strengthen assertions that detect each mutant; do not change source under test." The loop MUST terminate either by clearing the threshold or by the agent labelling each remaining mutant as `equivalent` with a one-line justification committed to `mutants.equivalent.md`.
rule.rationale: Without the structured surviving-mutants list, the agent re-runs coverage tactics that already failed. With it, the agent has unambiguous targets and converges in 1-3 iterations on real-world legacy retrofit benchmarks (Meta ACH: 73% merge rate at Messenger/WhatsApp).

example.code:
# After mutation CI fails: jq '.files | to_entries[] | .value.mutants[] | select(.status=="Survived") | {file:.location.start.line, mutator:.mutatorName, replacement}' \ reports/mutation/mutation.json > surviving.json
# Pass surviving.json to the agent as the next prompt context cat templates/mutation-prompt.txt surviving.json | claude -p
example.note: Structured input means the agent acts on rows, not vibes.

example.code: Prompt: "Mutation testing failed, please improve test coverage."
example.note: Agent cannot tell which mutants survived; falls back to weak assertions.

antipattern.description: Marking every surviving mutant as equivalent without justification to make CI green.
antipattern.reason: Equivalent-mutant claims must be auditable; an unreviewed `equivalent.md` is the same escape hatch as a coverage waiver.

section.reference: Prompt fragment that consumes the surviving-mutants list.
Meta ACH: LLM-driven mutation testing in production.
